design_decisions.choice: Read planned_files from committed status.json on release-wt ref via git show, cache per-track
design_decisions.rationale: Reading once at startup avoids repeated git operations; release-wt ref has planner-authored immutable specs. The git reader pattern already exists in parallel.go.

design_decisions.choice: Parse documented shared files from index.md markdown body (after frontmatter close), matching (DOCUMENTED SHARED) rows
design_decisions.rationale: The touchpoint matrix lives in the markdown body, not frontmatter. A regex-based row parser is simple, stdlib-only, and matches existing line-oriented parsing style.

design_decisions.choice: Block tracks at dispatch time (before goroutine launch) with follow-up phase after wg.Wait() for retry
design_decisions.rationale: The invariant-2 check is at dispatch time per spec. Blocked tracks retry after conflicting track finishes + auto-merges. Re-uses the phase-barrier pattern from S04 without new polling or goroutine patterns.

design_decisions.choice: Re-use existing S04 phase barrier for retry: follow-up phase launches blocked tracks only after all launched tracks finish
design_decisions.rationale: The spec says 'same retry mechanic as depends_on wait from S04', which is the phase barrier. finishTrack auto-merges on completion, so the follow-up phase's disjointness re-check passes naturally.

design_decisions.choice: Fail open when planned_files cannot be read (missing status.json, parse error, empty)
design_decisions.rationale: Spec AC-4 mandates fail-open: treat as zero planned files. A tight invariant-2 that blocks on missing data would prevent valid parallel runs.
